programmes.show_description: A security guard manages a violent incident with a patient. Jack, who's 33, is rushed into A&E after falling from a horse. And seven-year-old Lyla has a finger infection.
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 2/2): The issues keep coming at the Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont. Can Gordon help the staff and owners to communicate? Or will he be forced to abandon ship?
The epic adventure reality contest continues. Predator turns on Predator, but will it pay off? One player goes all in on their biggest threat, and a fiery clash sets rivalries alight. (Ep5)
The epic adventure reality contest continues. With five Predators and two Prey, the players face a brutal reset - and a shock decision delivers the biggest upset of the game so far. (Ep6)

programmes.show_name: Keir Starmer: Where Did It All...
programmes.show_description: ...Go Wrong? Dispatches: Journalist Lewis Goodall examines how the Starmer government became so unpopular so quickly, less than two years after the biggest election landslide in UK history.
In Cardiff and Newport, Rachel and Mark plan an Asian menu, Sue and Joyce serve 'posh' versions of classic dishes, including baked beans with chives, and there's a shock on the final night.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: This episode celebrates one of Kirstie's biggest builds as we look back at how Keeley and Terence completely overhauled their Woodford home beyond recognition.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Pick a Con... Any Con: Sam sees Coach being taken for a ride by a so-called friend, so he and the gang plot to get the money back from the con artist in a friendly poker game.
Someone Single, Someone Blue: Diane is told she must marry within 24 hours or her mother will lose an inheritance. So she asks Sam to marry her on a strictly business basis.
Showdown (Part 1/2): Sam's jealous when his brother shows up at the bar and captivates everyone - including a smitten Diane - with his tap-dancing, magic tricks and singing.
A Date for Peter: Amy's parents ask Ray to give their son Peter some tips on dating. Debra throws a cocktail party, but Ray isn't impressed by the guest list.
Favors: Marie helps Debra to deal with Ray's bad habit of leaving his stuff lying around the house.
The Faux Pas: Ray accidentally insults the twins' new friend... to the boy's father. If that's not bad enough, he makes things worse by repeating his mistake.
Taking Liberties: Frasier hires a butler, who spoils everyone and offers Frasier sage advice in his bid to join the snobbish opera board.
Legal Tender Love and Care: Frasier starts dating the attractive attorney who defended him against Donny - who was jilted at the altar by Daphne.
(2010) Family fantasy comedy. Dwayne Johnson stars as a tough ice hockey player who is transformed into a real-life fairy as punishment for telling a child that tooth fairies don't exist.
(2006) Family action-thriller with Alex Pettyfer and Mickey Rourke. British intelligence recruit a schoolboy to take on the man who killed his dad and is planning to unleash a deadly virus.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mike Bubbins in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Jaazzmina and Mark want to create a remote Spanish smallholding and wellness retreat in Almería Province. Can Sara Damergi find the right spot for £100,000?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, there's beer fear at Richard and Simon's new bar. Will they run out? A group of cyclists tackle the French Pyrenees' snowy mountains.
The new run concludes. After a feisty week in Kent - when some B&Bers couldn't even wait until payment day to get their gripes off their chest - it is now actually payment day.
The new run continues. In Sheffield, Ambrose hopes that his tried-and-tested dishes can bag him the grand. After a barrage of questions and competitive chat, has his night won out? (Ep5/5)
...the Sun: Jon's still out of action but it's full steam ahead as Jasmine and her dad work to complete the kids' bedrooms and family bathroom. Will daughter Joy approve of her room? (Ep5)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at Graham and Nardeth's worn-out Windsor home with a lacklustre layout. Did Kirstie's plan prevail, or did the list-it line-up win?
...Secrets Unwrapped: A fascinating look inside the Willy Wonka world of Cadbury across a make-or-break year, as they try to reduce the sugar in Dairy Milk and launch a Creme Egg campaign.
This time it's Race Across the World, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Last One Laughing, Secrets of the Bees, Just One Thing and Celebrity Ex on the Beach.
Josh Pugh, Lorraine Kelly and Steve Bracknall join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker for a tongue-in-cheek look at the week's news.
...Champions 2025: They've already won Greg's golden noggin. But which series supremo can go one better? Andy Zaltzman, John Robins, Maisie Adam, Mathew Baynton and Sam Campbell do battle.
Bill becomes the first British comedian to tread the boards of the Royal Opera House as he goes down the rabbit hole with Google reviews of Poundland, and Samuel Pepys in the modern age.
(2022) Tense thriller with Henry Thomas. A plumber gets trapped in the crawlspace of a house with a stash of cash that two bad guys want back. And they'll shoot blindly at walls to get it.
River Rock Inn: Gordon checks into the River Rock Inn in Pennsylvania, where owner Ken Pisciotta, who's drowning in debt, can't convince guests to stay in his neglected hotel.
Mother and daughter Linda and Kelly host first at The Old Times Guest House in Ipswich. They take their guests go-karting, but Kelly cuts corners with hot drink orders at breakfast.
The second visit is to The Flower Pot in Sunbury-on-Thames, where owner Simon and manager Pierre offer their guests a stylish stay and a game of real tennis at Hampton Court Palace.
The third visit is to the Dolphin Hotel in Exmouth, Devon, where owner Jo takes her guests paintballing and to a floating restaurant. But there are suspicions of game-playing.
Pat and Nigel pride themselves on their award-winning breakfast at their B&B in Seaton, Devon. But will an afternoon spent town-crying entertain their guests?
With all the visits over, the B&Bers come together to confront their rivals and discover how much they've been paid, before the winner is announced.
Kirstie Allsopp puts celebs to the test in crafting challenges. Chris Hollins and Laura Hamilton craft colourful paper gift bags.
Food Fight: Carrie's jealous when Doug falls for Spence's new girlfriend's excellent cooking.
Double Downer: Carrie and Doug go on an anniversary trip to Atlantic City. Unfortunately, Doug's boss is going too.
Dougie Nights: Doug and Deacon go clubbing, and Doug discovers that he enjoys it. Meanwhile, Carrie feels threatened by her new colleague, who looks just like her.
Tasteless Frank: Frank loses his sense of taste. Is it a side-effect of his potency medication? And will he have to tell Marie that he's receiving chemical assistance?
Sister-in-Law: Trouble brews in the Barone family when Robert's wife Amy finds out that Ray thinks she talks too much.
Moonshine River: Marge and Lisa try to experience all of New York City's culture when the family travel to the Big Apple looking for Bart's long-lost love Mary Spuckler.
Gone Abie Gone: Grampa goes missing. Can Homer and Marge find him by following clues from when he used to work in a restaurant?
(2021) Family animation. The plucky pups head to the city to foil dastardly Mayor Humdinger's latest plot. But can Chase overcome his fear of the big smoke and be the hero everyone needs?
The competition begins at Manor House Inn in Northumberland. Guests Tony and Paula are immediately enamoured of the honeymoon suite. But one B&Ber has a headboard headache.
The B&Bers embrace the great outdoors at Orchard Glamping in Lancashire. Hosts Tom and Becky hope their pristine pods are pitch perfect. But there's one unhappy camper in their midst.
Tony and Paula host the week's third visit at Hotel Ellenby in Scarborough, where they reckon stunning sea views and local history will be a winning combination. The breakfast, not so much.
The week's final visit is to The Crown Inn in Derbyshire, where a trip to an old mill proves to be inexplicably popular. More so than Dean's supposedly legendary breakfast, anyway...
Peace. Tranquillity. Relaxation. Unfortunately, it's payment day, so there's none of that here. Instead, there are slanderous sleep scores, bad breakfasts, and a no-show from two B&Bers!
Live coverage of the men's and women's races, as rowers from Cambridge and Oxford heave their way along the Thames, in one of the world's most prestigious amateur sporting events.
We catch up with Carole and Alan, who needed a home with lots of land and holiday let potential in Somerset or Devon. And did Ros and Hannah find the perfect first home in London?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or
programmes.show_description: List It: In Whitley Bay, Sarah, Sean and their five kids share six bedrooms - and one bathroom! Will knocking down walls fix things? Or should they leave their seaside spot?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Journeys: On the scenic Settle to Carlisle service, Mark prepares to pop the question to his partner, as executive chef Nick creates masterpiece meals onboard. (Ep2/4)
A passionate peek at local life on Yorkshire's stunning coastline. Fishing family the Robsons head to Whitby to repair their boat and we take a bracing dip in Scarborough's South Bay. (Ep1)
Bill goes deeper into ancient local tales, riding a junk boat through the breathtakingly beautiful limestone karsts of Ha Long Bay, where legends of dragons live on. (Ep6)
From polar bear raves to AI sea shanties and the collapse of the teaspoon industry, Bill leads a magical, musical mystery tour of the human mind in his 2025 stand-up at Edinburgh Playhouse.
(2022) It's Predator, 1719 style. Amber Midthunder stars as a Comanche hunter who must save her tribe from an extraterrestrial threat in this action prequel. If it bleeds, you can kill it.
